data.description:

See our latest analysis for JFE Holdings.

The earnings update comes after a strong 90 day share price return of 23.63% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 31.77%. However, recent softness, including a 7 day share price return decline of 4.09%, hints that momentum has cooled slightly.

For a cyclical group like JFE, focused on steel and engineering, that can reflect expectations around future profitability or the quality of its earnings stream.

data.title: Assessing Sabine Royalty Trust (SBR) Valuation After Lower Distribution And Weaker Oil And Gas Volumes
data.description:
Sabine Royalty Trust (SBR) drew fresh attention after Argent Trust Company declared a lower cash distribution of $0.283370 per unit, alongside February production figures that showed reduced oil and gas volumes versus the prior month.

See our latest analysis for Sabine Royalty Trust.

At a share price of $69.97, Sabine Royalty Trust has seen mixed momentum, with a 1 month share price return of 3.67% but a 90 day share price return decline of 10.02%, while its 1 year total shareholder return of 8.26% and 5 year total shareholder return that is very large point to stronger long term income plus price gains.

If the latest distribution cut has you thinking about where cash flows and income potential might be headed next, it could be a good moment to scan 22 elite gold producer stocks as another way to research resource linked plays.

With an intrinsic discount of about 49% and long term total returns that are very large, the key question now is whether recent production and distribution softness is already baked into the unit price, or if the trust still offers a mispriced income opportunity.

Advertisement

Preferred P/E of 12.9x: Is it justified?

At $69.97 per unit, Sabine Royalty Trust trades on a P/E of 12.9x, which our checks flag as good value against both peers and the wider US Oil and Gas group.

The P/E multiple compares what the market is paying today for each dollar of earnings, so for a royalty trust like Sabine it links unit price directly to the cash being generated by its underlying oil and gas interests. A 12.9x P/E, coupled with high quality earnings and an earnings growth rate of 13% per year over the past 5 years, suggests the market is not assigning a premium price to that track record.

Compared with the peer average P/E of 32.2x and the US Oil and Gas industry average of 14.5x, Sabine’s multiple sits at a clear discount. That gap, alongside an intrinsic discount of about 49% from our SWS DCF model, points to a valuation level the market could reassess if earnings and distributions remain supported by the underlying royalty stream.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 12.9x (UNDERVALUED)

What the SWS DCF model is implying

Our DCF model estimates a future cash flow value of $136.81 per unit for Sabine Royalty Trust, versus the recent $69.97 close, indicating a substantial valuation gap on a cash flow basis.

The SWS DCF model works by projecting the trust’s future cash flows, then discounting those back to today to reflect the time value of money and risk. For a royalty trust with high quality earnings and very large long term total returns, cash flow driven valuation can be particularly helpful because distributions are tied closely to commodity production and pricing.

In Sabine’s case, the combination of very large Return on Equity, high quality earnings and a long history of distributing cash to unitholders gives context to why the DCF output sits well above the current unit price, even after a year in which earnings declined by 16.2% and recent distributions have been lower.

data.title: NVIDIA Meta And India Deals Shape Global AI Infrastructure Story
data.description:
• NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) agreed a multi year, multigenerational partnership with Meta to supply millions of AI chips, along with GPUs, CPUs, networking and privacy technologies for use across Meta’s hyperscale data centers.
  • The company also announced a US$2b deployment with Yotta Data Services, involving 20,736 Blackwell Ultra GPUs to build what is described as Asia’s largest AI supercluster in India.
  • These agreements expand NVIDIA’s role in global AI infrastructure and position India as an emerging hub for high performance AI compute.

NVIDIA sits at the center of the data center and AI compute stack, selling chips and full platform solutions that power training and inference workloads. Partnerships with hyperscalers such as Meta and regional providers such as Yotta illustrate how demand for large scale AI clusters is spreading across both US and international markets. For investors, this highlights that AI infrastructure is being built out across multiple geographies and customer types, rather than being concentrated solely among a few US cloud providers.

Looking ahead, readers may want to watch how NVIDIA executes on these long duration supply commitments and how quickly partners ramp actual deployments. The scale of the Yotta build in India also brings attention to local regulatory, power, and data sovereignty considerations that may influence how efficiently these AI clusters are utilized.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for NVIDIA by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on NVIDIA.

2 things going right for NVIDIA that this headline doesn't cover.

For NVIDIA, the Meta and Yotta deals speak to two different but connected angles of the AI compute story. Meta’s commitment to use millions of current and future NVIDIA chips, plus Grace CPUs and networking, reinforces NVIDIA’s role as a full-stack supplier at hyperscale. That is important when rivals like AMD, Intel and in-house accelerators are all competing for data center sockets. The Yotta deployment in India shows how demand for high performance AI infrastructure is spreading into new regions, with over US$2b of Blackwell Ultra hardware plus a multi year DGX Cloud engagement anchoring NVIDIA inside an emerging AI hub rather than just selling chips into existing US data centers.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The NVIDIA Narrative

  • The long duration commitments from Meta and Yotta line up with the narrative that AI infrastructure buildouts underpin multi year demand visibility for NVIDIA’s platforms and software.
  • Concentration in a few very large customers, such as Meta, also echoes narrative concerns that hyperscalers developing custom silicon could eventually pressure NVIDIA’s pricing power or share if those efforts ramp.
  • The scale up in India through Yotta and other local partners adds a regional AI infrastructure angle that is only partly reflected in the existing narrative, which is more focused on US hyperscalers and sovereign projects elsewhere.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for NVIDIA to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that a high level of non cash earnings can make it harder to judge the quality and sustainability of NVIDIA’s reported profits.
  • ⚠️ Large, multi year GPU buildouts in India and elsewhere depend on reliable power, regulation and data sovereignty frameworks, which could slow deployments or limit utilization if conditions change.
  • 🎁 NVIDIA’s earnings grew by 57.5% over the past year, highlighting how AI data center demand has fed through to the bottom line.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 23.52% per year, and deals like Meta and Yotta add to the picture of ongoing demand for NVIDIA’s AI platforms.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch three things. First, how quickly Meta translates chip orders into live capacity and whether NVIDIA CPUs gain traction alongside GPUs in Meta’s data centers, where Intel and AMD have long competed. Second, the execution of the Yotta supercluster build toward its August 2026 go live target, including any updates on utilization by global model developers versus domestic IndiaAI workloads. Third, any signs that other hyperscalers or regional providers shift material AI spend toward alternative accelerators, as that would put these partnerships in a broader competitive context for NVIDIA’s data center business.

data.title: Companies Like Grace Therapeutics (NASDAQ:GRCE) Are In A Position To Invest In Growth
data.description:
Just because a business does not make any money, does not mean that the stock will go down. For example, although software-as-a-service business Salesforce.com lost money for years while it grew recurring revenue, if you held shares since 2005, you'd have done very well indeed. But the harsh reality is that very many loss making companies burn through all their cash and go bankrupt.

So should Grace Therapeutics (NASDAQ:GRCE) shareholders be worried about its cash burn? For the purposes of this article, cash burn is the annual rate at which an unprofitable company spends cash to fund its growth; its negative free cash flow. We'll start by comparing its cash burn with its cash reserves in order to calculate its cash runway.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Does Grace Therapeutics Have A Long Cash Runway?

You can calculate a company's cash runway by dividing the amount of cash it has by the rate at which it is spending that cash. When Grace Therapeutics last reported its December 2025 balance sheet in February 2026, it had zero debt and cash worth US$19m. In the last year, its cash burn was US$10m. That means it had a cash runway of around 22 months as of December 2025. That's not too bad, but it's fair to say the end of the cash runway is in sight, unless cash burn reduces drastically. You can see how its cash balance has changed over time in the image below.

See our latest analysis for Grace Therapeutics

How Is Grace Therapeutics' Cash Burn Changing Over Time?

Grace Therapeutics didn't record any revenue over the last year, indicating that it's an early stage company still developing its business. Nonetheless, we can still examine its cash burn trajectory as part of our assessment of its cash burn situation. While it hardly paints a picture of imminent growth, the fact that it has reduced its cash burn by 28% over the last year suggests some degree of prudence. While the past is always worth studying, it is the future that matters most of all. For that reason, it makes a lot of sense to take a look at our analyst forecasts for the company.

How Easily Can Grace Therapeutics Raise Cash?

While Grace Therapeutics is showing a solid reduction in its cash burn, it's still worth considering how easily it could raise more cash, even just to fuel faster growth. Issuing new shares, or taking on debt, are the most common ways for a listed company to raise more money for its business. One of the main advantages held by publicly listed companies is that they can sell shares to investors to raise cash and fund growth. By looking at a company's cash burn relative to its market capitalisation, we gain insight on how much shareholders would be diluted if the company needed to raise enough cash to cover another year's cash burn.

Since it has a market capitalisation of US$61m, Grace Therapeutics' US$10m in cash burn equates to about 17% of its market value. As a result, we'd venture that the company could raise more cash for growth without much trouble, albeit at the cost of some dilution.

Is Grace Therapeutics' Cash Burn A Worry?

The good news is that in our view Grace Therapeutics' cash burn situation gives shareholders real reason for optimism. Not only was its cash burn reduction quite good, but its cash runway was a real positive. While we're the kind of investors who are always a bit concerned about the risks involved with cash burning companies, the metrics we have discussed in this article leave us relatively comfortable about Grace Therapeutics' situation. data.title: Be Sure To Check Out United Fire Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:UFCS) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend
data.description:
United Fire Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:UFCS) stock is about to trade ex-dividend in 4 days. The ex-dividend date occurs one day before the record date, which is the day on which shareholders need to be on the company's books in order to receive a dividend. It is important to be aware of the ex-dividend date because any trade on the stock needs to have been settled on or before the record date. Therefore, if you purchase United Fire Group's shares on or after the 24th of February, you won't be eligible to receive the dividend, when it is paid on the 10th of March.

The company's next dividend payment will be US$0.20 per share, on the back of last year when the company paid a total of US$0.64 to shareholders. Last year's total dividend payments show that United Fire Group has a trailing yield of 2.1% on the current share price of US$38.66. Dividends are a major contributor to investment returns for long term holders, but only if the dividend continues to be paid. That's why we should always check whether the dividend payments appear sustainable, and if the company is growing.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Dividends are typically paid out of company income, so if a company pays out more than it earned, its dividend is usually at a higher risk of being cut. United Fire Group is paying out just 14% of its profit after tax, which is comfortably low and leaves plenty of breathing room in the case of adverse events.

Companies that pay out less in dividends than they earn in profits generally have more sustainable dividends. The lower the payout ratio, the more wiggle room the business has before it could be forced to cut the dividend.

See our latest analysis for United Fire Group

Click here to see how much of its profit United Fire Group paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Stocks in companies that generate sustainable earnings growth often make the best dividend prospects, as it is easier to lift the dividend when earnings are rising. If earnings decline and the company is forced to cut its dividend, investors could watch the value of their investment go up in smoke. It's encouraging to see United Fire Group has grown its earnings rapidly, up 37% a year for the past five years.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. It looks like the United Fire Group dividends are largely the same as they were 10 years ago.

To Sum It Up

Has United Fire Group got what it takes to maintain its dividend payments? Typically, companies that are growing rapidly and paying out a low fraction of earnings are keeping the profits for reinvestment in the business. This strategy can add significant value to shareholders over the long term - as long as it's done without issuing too many new shares. We think this is a pretty attractive combination, and would be interested in investigating United Fire Group more closely.

While it's tempting to invest in United Fire Group for the dividends alone, you should always be mindful of the risks involved. To help with this, we've discovered 1 warning sign for United Fire Group that you should be aware of before investing in their shares.

data.title: Will Aware (NASDAQ:AWRE) Spend Its Cash Wisely?
data.description:
There's no doubt that money can be made by owning shares of unprofitable businesses. For example, biotech and mining exploration companies often lose money for years before finding success with a new treatment or mineral discovery. Nonetheless, only a fool would ignore the risk that a loss making company burns through its cash too quickly.

So, the natural question for Aware (NASDAQ:AWRE) shareholders is whether they should be concerned by its rate of cash burn. In this article, we define cash burn as its annual (negative) free cash flow, which is the amount of money a company spends each year to fund its growth. First, we'll determine its cash runway by comparing its cash burn with its cash reserves.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

When Might Aware Run Out Of Money?

A company's cash runway is the amount of time it would take to burn through its cash reserves at its current cash burn rate. When Aware last reported its September 2025 balance sheet in October 2025, it had zero debt and cash worth US$23m. Looking at the last year, the company burnt through US$5.8m. That means it had a cash runway of about 3.9 years as of September 2025. There's no doubt that this is a reassuringly long runway. The image below shows how its cash balance has been changing over the last few years.

View our latest analysis for Aware

How Well Is Aware Growing?

It was quite stunning to see that Aware increased its cash burn by 605% over the last year. That does give us pause, and we can't take much solace in the operating revenue growth of 2.7% in the same time frame. In light of the above-mentioned, we're pretty wary of the trajectory the company seems to be on. Of course, we've only taken a quick look at the stock's growth metrics, here. You can take a look at how Aware has developed its business over time by checking this visualization of its revenue and earnings history.

How Hard Would It Be For Aware To Raise More Cash For Growth?

While Aware seems to be in a fairly good position, it's still worth considering how easily it could raise more cash, even just to fuel faster growth. Generally speaking, a listed business can raise new cash through issuing shares or taking on debt. Commonly, a business will sell new shares in itself to raise cash and drive growth. We can compare a company's cash burn to its market capitalisation to get a sense for how many new shares a company would have to issue to fund one year's operations.

Aware has a market capitalisation of US$36m and burnt through US$5.8m last year, which is 16% of the company's market value. As a result, we'd venture that the company could raise more cash for growth without much trouble, albeit at the cost of some dilution.

So, Should We Worry About Aware's Cash Burn?

Even though its increasing cash burn makes us a little nervous, we are compelled to mention that we thought Aware's cash runway was relatively promising. Cash burning companies are always on the riskier side of things, but after considering all of the factors discussed in this short piece, we're not too worried about its rate of cash burn. Its important for readers to be cognizant of the risks that can affect the company's operations, and we've picked out 1 warning sign for Aware that investors should know when investing in the stock.

data.title: BlackRock Ethereum ETF Staking Move Draws Institutions And New Fee Stream
data.description:
• BlackRock, ticker NYSE:BLK, is moving ahead with its Ethereum ETF, ETHB, which is structured to pass staking rewards through to fund investors.
  • The product is described as a first of its kind in the US market for giving ETF holders direct exposure to Ethereum staking economics.
  • Institutional investors including Harvard Management Company and Abu Dhabi's Mubadala are reported to be shifting allocations from Bitcoin ETFs into BlackRock's Ethereum offerings.

BlackRock, ticker NYSE:BLK, is already a major player in traditional ETFs and index products, and it is now expanding that footprint in digital assets with Ethereum focused funds. By linking ETHB to staking rewards, the firm is adding a feature that has typically been available only to investors holding tokens directly, but now within a regulated, exchange traded wrapper.

For you as an investor, the key takeaway is that large institutions are not just looking at whether to own digital assets; they are also choosing between different ways to access them. As Ethereum products such as ETHB gain traction, the mix of institutional flows between Bitcoin and Ethereum could keep evolving, and BlackRock's role in offering these vehicles may remain an important reference point for how regulated crypto exposure develops.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for BlackRock by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on BlackRock.

2 things going right for BlackRock that this headline doesn't cover.

This Ethereum ETF move reinforces BlackRock’s push to be a key access point for digital assets, similar to what it already does with equity and bond ETFs. By passing through about 82% of staking rewards and retaining 18% with Coinbase, the firm is effectively creating a new fee line tied to Ethereum network activity, rather than just price exposure. The reported rotation by investors such as Harvard Management Company and Mubadala from Bitcoin products into BlackRock’s Ethereum vehicles also shows that the firm is capturing both sides of the allocation decision, not just competing for assets with rivals like Fidelity and Invesco. For you, the practical angle is that BlackRock is building a broader “menu” of regulated crypto exposures that can sit alongside traditional ETFs in multi-asset portfolios. The key questions are whether staking-based products see sustained demand from institutions with strict risk and compliance requirements, and how fee sharing on staking compares with economics available elsewhere in crypto and in traditional higher-fee products.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The BlackRock Narrative

  • The Ethereum ETF and staking structure align with BlackRock’s focus on product innovation and digital assets as an additional growth channel alongside ETFs, alternatives, and technology platforms.
  • Expanding into staking and tokenized exposures could add operational and regulatory complexity, which the existing narrative already flags as a potential headwind from higher costs and integration risk.
  • The rotation of institutional capital into Ethereum products and the staking-fee revenue stream are not explicitly detailed in the narrative, so they may represent an extra lever within the broader digital-asset opportunity that investors are still sizing.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for BlackRock to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Increased exposure to Ethereum, including staking, adds another layer of regulatory and technological risk on top of BlackRock’s existing ETF and alternatives businesses.
  • ⚠️ Concentrating more activity with a single staking partner such as Coinbase could raise counterparty and operational risk if market stress or technical issues occur.
  • 🎁 Analysts have flagged that earnings are forecast to grow 12.84% per year, and new fee sources from products like ETHB may contribute to that growth if investor interest is sustained.
  • 🎁 Analysts also highlight that the stock is trading below their price targets, and wider digital-asset offerings can support the case that BlackRock continues to attract flows across traditional and crypto products.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how quickly assets build in ETHB once it lists on Nasdaq, how much of BlackRock’s Ethereum under management is actually staked, and whether other issuers such as Fidelity or VanEck launch similar reward-sharing ETFs that pressure fees. You may also want to track disclosures from large holders like Harvard and Mubadala to see if reallocations between Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs continue, as this will influence the mix of crypto-related revenue BlackRock earns. Finally, any SEC or other regulatory commentary around staking inside regulated funds will be important, because it could shape how scalable this product line is for BlackRock over time.
